spec_match.rationale: The page now applies real CSS to browser-computed var() and attr() probes, including skipped fallbacks, taken fallbacks, cycles, and invalid substituted values.

interactive.rationale: Preset buttons, a target declaration selector, editable custom properties, and attr() controls all update live rendered output and computed-result readouts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index promises a snippet-driven cycle detector with clean, fallback-only, and primary-cycle cases; the page now delivers that plus attr() coverage.

edge_cases.rationale: Visible branches cover a skipped cyclic fallback, a taken cyclic fallback, a primary-path cycle, an invalid token stream after substitution, nested fallback short-circuiting, and missing or empty attr() values.

references.rationale: The see-also section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6212939656462336.

design_system.rationale: Inline CSS uses shared design variables for colors, local button styles pair background with color, and the concept is a live tool rather than a static card.

summary: Cycle Detector now runs browser-computed CSS for var() and attr() fallback behavior and surfaces both the short-circuiting success path and the visible invalid edge cases.
